zotero.abstractNote: Probabilistic numerical methods for ordinary differential equations (ODEs) provide a quantification of the uncertainty arising from the discretization of the continuous problem. However, existing methods often suffer from poor calibration: the reported uncertainty does not accurately reflect the true error. We propose a new class of adaptive probabilistic ODE solvers that are calibrated by construction. We achieve this by interpreting the step-size control mechanism as a Bayesian filtering problem. This allows us to derive a step-size adaptation rule that is consistent with the probabilistic model of the solution. We demonstrate that our method provides reliable uncertainty estimates and competitive performance on a range of benchmark problems.
zotero.conferenceName: 24th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 24th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
